Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff Will Fredericks Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT FELTER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff Will Fredericks Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBERT FELTER's Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff Will Fredericks Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant /Cross-complainant Robert Felter's Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions To Plaintiff Will Fredericks Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Under CCP 2033.280(c), the court shall deem requests for admissions (RFAs) admitted, unless it finds the responding party, before hearing on the motion, served a proposed response to the requests for admission that is in substantial compliance with CCP 2033.220. Here, it is undisputed Plaintiff Will Fredericks served substantially compliant, verified responses while this motion was pending. Thus, the request for an order establishing admissions is denied.

As for Defendant's sanctions request, the court must impose monetary sanctions on the party or attorney whose failure to serve a timely response necessitates the motion. (CCP 2033.280(c).) Here, Plaintiff failed to serve timely responses. The court orders Plaintiff Will Fredericks and his counsel Jeffrey Woo and Womble Bond Dickinson (US) LLP, jointly and severally, to pay Felter $1,500 in sanctions by August 29, 2025.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Continued To Jul-15-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC AND SCOTT BRITTAIN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CONTINUED to July 15, 2025 at 9:30 a.m., but the Court intends to rule as follows: The Demurrer is SUSTAINED with and without leave to amend. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend the negligence cause of action. Compass must allege a duty Sotheby's owed to them, not to the buyer or seller. To the extent Compass is relying on Civil Code section 2079.16, that section provides that a buyer's agent owes duties to the to the buyer and seller, not to their agents. Compass cannot bring a claim for negligence against Sotheby's on Buyers' or Seller's behalf.

The demurrer to the equitable indemnity and contribution causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. Contrary to Compass' assertions, the court must look to Felter's claims against Compass to determine whether Sotheby's can be jointly liable to Felter on Felter's theory of liability. Compass' reliance on Leko v. Cornerstone Bldg. Inspection Service (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1109 and their use of the word "plaintiff" is misplaced. In Leko, a home purchaser sued the seller and both sets of real estate agents (buyer's agent and seller's agent) for failing to disclose structural defects caused by an earthquake. Both agents then cross-complained for equitable indemnity against several home inspection companies who had not been sued by the home purchaser. (Id. at 1113.) Here, Plaintiffs did not sue Compass; Felter sued Compass. Thus, if Compass is to be indemnified by Sotheby's, it would be because they were jointly liable to Felter (not plaintiffs/buyers).

The Court does not find any facts that could support shifting responsibility from Compass to Sotheby's for Compass' advice to their own client of which reports to include in the disclosures. The Court does not see how Compass can cure their indemnity or contribution causes of action, but invites Compass to come prepared with argument and authority on why the Court is incorrect in its interpretation of the law. Compass may file a supplemental brief, no longer than 2 pages due by July 14, 2025 at 4:00 p.m., of supporting authority that has not already been cited in their opposition. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Peter Geissler?S Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Jun-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS WILL FREDERICKS, and SHANNON FREDERICKS' Motion To Compel Defendant Peter Geissler's Responses To Discovery.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Felter sold his San Francisco home with the assistance and involvement of cross-defendant Compass Realty (and named brokers) and defendant Peter Geissler, a civil engineer. Geissler was retained to inspect the home's foundation. He opined that the foundation was stable and defect-free. Plaintiffs then entered into a sales agreement and purchased title to the home. As it turned out, the home's foundation was allegedly defective, and plaintiffs brought this action against Felter and Geissler, with cross-actions bringing in Compass Realty and its brokers. Plaintiffs contend they served written discovery on defendant Geissler who is not represented by counsel. The file contains an email from Geissler seemingly acknowledging receipt of the written discovery and promising to provide responses. However, no responses were served, and plaintiffs have brought this motion for an order compelling Geissler to provide verified responses without objection. Although the file does not contain a proof of service of the discovery on Geissler, his email does appear to acknowledge its receipt. Accordingly, his failure to provide timely responses entitled plaintiffs to an order compelling those responses, without objections and with a verification. Plaintiffs' motion for such an order is GRANTED. Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare an order consistent with the foregoing providing that defendant Geissler shall respond to the three discovery devices within 20 days of the file date of the order. That order shall be sent to psw@hassard.com for review and execution.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Cross-Complainant Robert Felter'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The First Affirmative Defense Of The Amended Answer Of Cross-Defendants Compass California Ii, Inc., Pete Brannigan, And Silvia Zeng
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

James B. Kraus; trn Law Associates; 415-823-4566; appearing as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant Robert Felter.
Jessica Beeler; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 619-233-1006; appearing as counsel for cross-defendants Compass California II, Inc. dba Compass, Pete Brannigan and Silvia Zeng.

Both Mr. Kraus and Ms. Beeler contest the court's tentative decision. Mr. Kraus requests that the court rule on cross-complainant's Request for Judicial Notice. The court denies cross-complainant's Request for Judicial Notice as to exhibits 8-10. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-COMPLAINANT ROBERT FELTER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF THE AMENDED ANSWER OF CROSS-DEFENDANTS COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC., PETE BRANNIGAN, AND SILVIA ZENG is GRANTED as to the purported first affirmative defense as it alleges no new facts constituting an affirmative defense (Protect Our Benefits v. City and County of San Francisco (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 619, 638) The Court notes that this motion does not advance this matter in any meaningful way as the sufficiency of the allegations in the operative Cross-Complaint cannot be addressed by challenging the sufficiency of the allegations regarding an affirmative defense stating to the contrary. The sufficiency of the allegations in the operative Cross-Complaint are not before this Court on this motion re: Answer to Cross-Complaint. It remains the Cross-Complainant's burden to plead and prove their case whether the purported first affirmative defense remains in the answer or not.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Jeanine Farrell, CSR #7774, depos@westcoastreporting.net, 415-472-2361. (D501)
